---
title: "Policy Impact Register"
subtitle: "Where the research is landing — tracked by paper, citing organization, and geographic reach."
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: [cosmo, theme.scss]
    orientation: rows
    nav-buttons: []
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.io as pio
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# ROOT CAUSE of the duplicate-chart bug (confirmed by inspecting the actual
# rendered dashboard.html): Quarto's Jupyter engine sets
# ast_node_interactivity="all" so every top-level expression in a cell
# auto-displays (mirroring R's print-everything semantics) — not just the
# final one. Plotly's fig.update_traces()/update_layout() both return the
# Figure itself, and a bare Figure object has its own rich repr that
# renders as a full interactive widget. So a chunk like:
#     fig.update_traces(...)   # <- bare expression, auto-displayed too!
#     fig.update_layout(...)   # <- same
#     show_fig(fig)            # <- the one output we actually want
# was producing 2-3 outputs per cell, not 1. A trailing `;` does NOT fix
# this — IPython's semicolon-suppression only applies to a cell's FINAL
# statement, not intermediate ones. The actual fix: capture the return
# value via assignment (`fig = fig.update_layout(...)`) so it's an Assign
# node, not a bare Expr node — assignments are never auto-displayed.
# Every update_traces/update_layout call below follows that pattern.
from IPython.display import Image
# Charts render to static PNG (via Plotly's kaleido export) rather than
# interactive widgets — this also sidesteps the auto-display issue above
# for the final, intended output. Static export requires Chrome; if you
# don't already have it, run this once: `plotly_get_chrome`
def show_fig(fig, width=900, height=None, **kwargs):
    h = height or fig.layout.height or 400
    path = f"/tmp/_fig_{abs(hash((width, h, str(fig.layout.title))))}_{id(fig)}.png"
    fig.write_image(path, width=width, height=h, scale=2)
    return Image(path)
import os, time, requests

# ---- 1. LOAD ------------------------------------------------------------
# Re-export this CSV from Policy Profiles / Overton and drop it in the
# same folder as this file to refresh the dashboard, then re-run
# `quarto render dashboard.qmd`.
DATA_PATH = "policy-impact-export-2026-06-18.csv"

# ---- 1b. OPTIONAL — live refresh via the Overton API --------------------
# Sage Policy Profiles itself has no public API — it's a free, browser
# tool that assembles your export client-side, which is why the download
# you get from it is a session-local `blob:` URL rather than a normal
# link. But it's built on Overton, and Overton DOES have a documented
# REST API for accounts with API access enabled (institutional
# subscribers — see https://app.overton.io/swagger.php). If you have an
# Overton API key, this block will pull live data straight from there
# instead of the static CSV.
#
# 1. Get your key: in the Overton app, Export -> "Generate API call".
# 2. Set it as an environment variable (never hardcode it in this file):
#       export OVERTON_API_KEY="your-key-here"
# 3. List every DOI of your own papers you want tracked below. These
#    five are just the ones already present in the CSV, as a starting
#    point — add any of your other papers too.
MY_DOIS = [
    "10.1038/s41586-022-05316-6",
    "10.1016/j.oneear.2020.07.012",
    "10.1016/j.enpol.2013.09.021",
    "10.1038/s41467-020-16184-x",
    "10.1038/s43017-021-00244-x",
    # ...add the rest of your DOIs here
]
OVERTON_API_KEY = os.environ.get("OVERTON_API_KEY")

def fetch_overton_citations(dois, api_key, per_doi_pause=1.0):
    """Pull every policy document citing each DOI in `dois` via Overton's
    documented `plain_dois_cited` endpoint, paginating with next_page_url.
    Returns a DataFrame shaped like the Policy Profiles CSV export.

    NOTE: the result schema below covers the fields Overton's docs show
    explicitly (title, translated_title, source.title/country/type,
    policy_document_id). Fields like the publication date, document URL,
    and topics weren't shown in full in the public docs snippet — print
    one raw `r` below to confirm the exact keys for your account, and
    adjust the `.get(...)` calls if the names differ.
    """
    rows = []
    for doi in dois:
        url = "https://app.overton.io/documents.php"
        params = {"plain_dois_cited": doi, "format": "json", "api_key": api_key}
        while True:
            resp = requests.get(url, params=params, timeout=30)
            if resp.status_code == 429:
                time.sleep(2)
                continue
            resp.raise_for_status()
            payload = resp.json()
            for r in payload.get("results", []):
                src = r.get("source", {})
                rows.append({
                    "Type": "Policy citation",
                    "Policy Document ID": r.get("policy_document_id"),
                    "Title": r.get("title"),
                    "Translated title": r.get("translated_title"),
                    "Source title": src.get("title"),
                    "Source country": src.get("country"),
                    "Source type": src.get("type"),
                    "Source sub-type": None,
                    "Published on": r.get("date") or r.get("published_on"),
                    "Cited research titles": r.get("cited_title"),
                    "Cited research DOIs": doi,
                    "Topics": r.get("topics"),
                    "Document URL": r.get("document_url"),
                    "Text": "",
                })
            next_url = payload.get("query", {}).get("next_page_url")
            if not next_url:
                break
            url, params = next_url, {}
            time.sleep(per_doi_pause)
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)

df = None
if OVERTON_API_KEY:
    try:
        df = fetch_overton_citations(MY_DOIS, OVERTON_API_KEY)
        if df.empty:
            df = None
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Overton API fetch failed ({e}); falling back to the CSV export.")
        df = None

if df is None:
    df = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH, encoding="utf-8-sig")

df["Published on"] = pd.to_datetime(df["Published on"], errors="coerce")

GOLD, TEAL, BRICK, MUTED = "#D4A24C", "#3FA092", "#C1654A", "#93A0C2"
INK, CARD = "#121A30", "#1B2747"
PALETTE = [GOLD, TEAL, BRICK, MUTED, "#7C8DB5", "#E6C27A"]

pio.templates["registry"] = go.layout.Template(
    layout=go.Layout(
        paper_bgcolor=CARD, plot_bgcolor=CARD,
        font=dict(family="Inter, sans-serif", color="#F4F0E6", size=12),
        colorway=PALETTE,
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.07)", zerolinecolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.07)", automargin=True),
        yaxis=dict(gridcolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.07)", zerolinecolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.07)", automargin=True),
        margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=30, b=10),
    )
)
pio.templates.default = "registry"

# ---- 2. CLEAN / EXPLODE -------------------------------------------------
def split_field(s):
    if pd.isna(s):
        return []
    return [x.strip() for x in s.split("，") if x.strip()]

# Cited papers: the export uses a full-width comma to separate multiple
# papers in one cell, but some single titles also contain a literal comma
# (re-encoded the same way) — so only split when the DOI list confirms
# there's more than one paper in that cell.
paper_rows = []
for _, r in df.iterrows():
    dois = split_field(r["Cited research DOIs"])
    traw = r["Cited research titles"]
    if pd.isna(traw):
        continue
    titles = [traw.replace("  ，", ",").strip()] if len(dois) <= 1 else split_field(traw)
    for i, t in enumerate(titles):
        doi = dois[i] if i < len(dois) else (dois[0] if len(dois) == 1 else None)
        paper_rows.append({"paper": t, "doi": doi})
papers_df = pd.DataFrame(paper_rows)
top_papers = (
    papers_df.groupby("paper")
    .agg(citations=("paper", "count"), doi=("doi", "first"))
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("citations", ascending=False)
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

orgs_df = (
    df.groupby(["Source title", "Source country", "Source type"])
    .agg(citations=("Source title", "count"), latest=("Published on", "max"),
         doc_url=("Document URL", "first"))
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"Source title": "org", "Source country": "country", "Source type": "source_type"})
    .sort_values("citations", ascending=False)
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

all_topics = []
for s in df["Topics"].dropna():
    all_topics.extend(split_field(s))
topics_s = pd.Series(all_topics).value_counts().reset_index()
topics_s.columns = ["topic", "count"]

tl = df.dropna(subset=["Published on"]).copy()
tl["year"] = tl["Published on"].dt.year.astype("Int64").astype(str)
timeline = tl.groupby("year").size().reset_index(name="citations")

country_df = df["Source country"].value_counts().reset_index()
country_df.columns = ["country", "citations"]

type_df = df["Source type"].value_counts().reset_index()
type_df.columns = ["source_type", "citations"]

n_total = len(df)
n_orgs = df["Source title"].nunique()
n_countries = df["Source country"].nunique()
n_papers = top_papers.shape[0]

def short(s, n=34):
    """Truncate long category labels for axis display (full text still
    shows on hover via Plotly's default hovertemplate on the raw column)."""
    s = str(s)
    return s if len(s) <= n else s[: n - 1].rstrip() + "…"
```

# Overview

## Row {height="16%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "POLICY MENTIONS"
dict(icon="file-earmark-text", color="#1B2747", value=int(n_total))
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "CITING ORGANIZATIONS"
dict(icon="buildings", color="#1B2747", value=int(n_orgs))
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "COUNTRIES & IGOs REACHED"
dict(icon="globe-americas", color="#1B2747", value=int(n_countries))
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "PAPERS WITH POLICY REACH"
dict(icon="journal-text", color="#1B2747", value=int(n_papers))
```

## Row {height="42%"}

### Column {width="38%"}

```{python}
#| title: "MENTIONS BY SOURCE TYPE"
fig = px.pie(type_df, names="source_type", values="citations", hole=0.55,
             color="source_type",
             color_discrete_map={"think tank": GOLD, "igo": TEAL, "government": BRICK, "other": MUTED})
fig = fig.update_traces(textfont_size=12, textinfo="label+value")
fig = fig.update_layout(showlegend=False, height=300)
show_fig(fig, width=520)
```

### Column {width="62%"}

```{python}
#| title: "MENTIONS BY COUNTRY / IGO"
top_c = country_df.head(12).sort_values("citations").copy()
top_c["country_disp"] = top_c["country"].apply(short)
fig = px.bar(top_c, x="citations", y="country_disp", orientation="h", hover_name="country")
fig = fig.update_traces(marker_color=GOLD)
fig = fig.update_layout(height=300, yaxis_title="", xaxis_title="")
show_fig(fig, width=850)
```

## Row {height="42%"}

```{python}
#| title: "TOP CITING ORGANIZATIONS"
top_o = orgs_df.head(12).sort_values("citations").copy()
top_o["org_disp"] = top_o["org"].apply(short)
fig = px.bar(top_o, x="citations", y="org_disp", orientation="h", color="source_type", hover_name="org",
             color_discrete_map={"think tank": GOLD, "igo": TEAL, "government": BRICK, "other": MUTED})
fig = fig.update_layout(height=320, yaxis_title="", xaxis_title="", legend_title="")
show_fig(fig, width=1300)
```

# By Paper

## Row {height="55%"}

```{python}
#| title: "CITATIONS PER PAPER"
tp = top_papers.sort_values("citations").copy()
tp["paper_disp"] = tp["paper"].apply(lambda s: short(s, 55))
fig = px.bar(tp, x="citations", y="paper_disp", orientation="h", hover_name="paper")
fig = fig.update_traces(marker_color=GOLD)
fig = fig.update_layout(height=420, yaxis_title="", xaxis_title="", margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=20, b=10))
show_fig(fig, width=1300)
```

## Row {height="45%"}

```{python}
#| title: "PAPER REGISTRY — RANKED BY POLICY CITATIONS"
from IPython.display import HTML
disp = top_papers.copy()
disp["DOI"] = disp["doi"].apply(
    lambda d: f'<a href="https://doi.org/{d}" target="_blank">{d}</a>' if pd.notna(d) else "—"
)
disp = disp.rename(columns={"paper": "Cited paper", "citations": "Citations"})[["Cited paper", "DOI", "Citations"]]
HTML(disp.to_html(index=False, escape=False))
```

# By Organization

## Row {height="55%"}

### Column {width="55%"}

```{python}
#| title: "REACH BY ORGANIZATION (TOP 15)"
to = orgs_df.head(15).sort_values("citations").copy()
to["org_disp"] = to["org"].apply(short)
fig = px.bar(to, x="citations", y="org_disp", orientation="h", color="source_type", hover_name="org",
             color_discrete_map={"think tank": GOLD, "igo": TEAL, "government": BRICK, "other": MUTED})
fig = fig.update_layout(height=420, yaxis_title="", xaxis_title="", legend_title="")
show_fig(fig, width=750)
```

### Column {width="45%"}

```{python}
#| title: "ORGANIZATION TYPE × CITATIONS"
fig = px.treemap(orgs_df, path=["source_type", "org"], values="citations",
                  color="source_type",
                  color_discrete_map={"think tank": GOLD, "igo": TEAL, "government": BRICK, "other": MUTED})
fig = fig.update_traces(textfont_size=11)
fig = fig.update_layout(height=420, margin=dict(l=4, r=4, t=20, b=4))
show_fig(fig, width=620)
```

## Row {height="45%"}

```{python}
#| title: "ORGANIZATION REGISTRY"
from IPython.display import HTML
disp = orgs_df.copy()
disp["Most recent citation"] = disp["latest"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
disp["Document"] = disp["doc_url"].apply(
    lambda u: f'<a href="{u}" target="_blank">link</a>' if pd.notna(u) else "—"
)
disp = disp.rename(columns={"org": "Organization", "country": "Country", "source_type": "Type", "citations": "Citations"})
disp = disp[["Organization", "Country", "Type", "Citations", "Most recent citation", "Document"]]
HTML(disp.to_html(index=False, escape=False))
```

# Topics & Reach

## Row {height="50%"}

### Column {width="55%"}

```{python}
#| title: "MOST-LINKED TOPICS"
tt = topics_s.head(15).sort_values("count").copy()
tt["topic_disp"] = tt["topic"].apply(short)
fig = px.bar(tt, x="count", y="topic_disp", orientation="h", hover_name="topic")
fig = fig.update_traces(marker_color=TEAL)
fig = fig.update_layout(height=380, yaxis_title="", xaxis_title="")
show_fig(fig, width=750)
```

### Column {width="45%"}

```{python}
#| title: "POLICY MENTIONS OVER TIME (BY YEAR)"
fig = px.area(timeline, x="year", y="citations")
fig = fig.update_traces(line_color=GOLD, fillcolor="rgba(212,162,76,0.18)")
fig = fig.update_layout(height=380, xaxis_title="", yaxis_title="")
show_fig(fig, width=620)
```

## Row {height="50%"}

```{python}
#| title: "MENTION TYPE — POLICY CITATION VS. NAME MENTION"
td = df["Type"].value_counts().reset_index()
td.columns = ["type", "count"]
fig = px.bar(td, x="count", y="type", orientation="h", color="type",
             color_discrete_map={"Policy citation": GOLD, "Name mention": MUTED})
fig = fig.update_layout(height=220, yaxis_title="", xaxis_title="", showlegend=False)
show_fig(fig, width=1300)
```
